first_run_note: This is the initial pipeline run. No previous data exists for comparison. Staleness and regression checks are not applicable. Coverage gaps reflect the bootstrap state of the system.

critical_gaps.military_section: 1.3% filled (3/75 countries) — only event-extracted defense spending
critical_gaps.exchange_rates: 0% mapped — data collected by Agent 02 but not mapped to country files
critical_gaps.equity_indices: Only 14/75 countries (18.7%) — concentrated in Tier 1 major markets

coverage.note: All 60 countries are below target thresholds. This is expected for the first run: 3 entire sections (political, trade, military) are empty. The filled sections (macroeconomic 77.1%, demographic 100%, derived 100%) show strong collection. Effective coverage of collected sections: ~85% for Tier 1, ~78% for Tier 2, ~76% for Tier 3.

staleness.note: First pipeline run (2026-W09). All data freshly collected on 2026-03-02. Staleness check not applicable — no pre-existing data.

confidence.note: No countries below 0.3 confidence threshold. Overall confidence is strong (0.805) with macroeconomic data at 0.876 (high — from official/authoritative sources) and derived metrics at 0.642 (moderate — relies on estimates due to incomplete base data). Confidence will improve as more source layers are added.

validation.data_quality_assessment: HIGH — All 1,095 factor updates pass plausibility checks. Zero rejections. Two flagged anomalies are confirmed plausible (Libya GDP growth, Hormuz traffic collapse). Six escalation groups driven by genuinely extraordinary geopolitical events, not data quality issues.

flagged_details.notes: Pre-flagged anomaly from processing agent: IMF WEO NGDP_RPCH estimate for 2025 [ANOMALY: extreme GDP growth value]; Libya 15.6% GDP growth is extreme but plausible: post-conflict oil production recovery from low base. IMF WEO estimate.
Pre-flagged anomaly from processing agent: Strait of Hormuz vessel traffic collapsed ~70% following US-Israel strikes on Iran; ~170 containerships trapped

pipeline_timing.bottleneck: Agent 09 (data_integrator) at 168 minutes — 17x over its 10-minute budget. First-run initialization overhead. Also significant idle time between Analysis sub-phases (Agent 10 end to Agent 11 start: 192 minutes gap).

recommendations.action: Add political/governance data collection to gathering agents
recommendations.reason: Political section is 0% filled for all 75 countries. Agent 06 (political_regulatory_gatherer) collects events but does not produce structured governance indices (political_stability_index, government_effectiveness, corruption_perceptions_index, democracy_index, rule_of_law_index, regulatory_quality). These are available from World Bank WGI, Freedom House, Transparency International, and EIU.
recommendations.impact: Would increase coverage by ~16% across all tiers.

recommendations.action: Add trade data collection to gathering agents
recommendations.reason: Trade section is 0% filled for all 75 countries. trade_openness_pct, top_export_partners, top_import_partners, export_concentration_index are not being collected. UN Comtrade and World Bank data are available.
recommendations.impact: Would increase coverage by ~11% across all tiers.

recommendations.action: Add military data collection beyond defense spending
recommendations.reason: Military section has only 3 of 75 countries with any data (USA, DEU, POL — from SIPRI/event extraction). defense_spending_pct_gdp, active_military_personnel, nuclear_capability are missing for nearly all countries. SIPRI and IISS data are available.
recommendations.impact: Would increase coverage by ~10% across all tiers.

recommendations.action: Expand exchange rate collection in Agent 02
recommendations.reason: exchange_rate_usd is missing for all 75 countries. Agent 02 collected 30 exchange rates as raw data but they were not mapped to country files as exchange_rate_usd field. Check Agent 07 mapping logic.
recommendations.impact: Data exists in raw collection — mapping fix would immediately add 1 factor for all countries.

recommendations.action: Expand equity index and bond yield collection for Tier 1 countries
recommendations.reason: equity_index_level missing for 17 of 30 Tier 1 countries (only 13 collected). sovereign_bond_yield_10yr_pct missing for 5 of 30 Tier 1 countries (ARE, CHN, MYS, SAU, THA). These are major investable markets.
recommendations.impact: Critical for investor-focused platform — these are high-frequency, high-value factors.

recommendations.action: Expand central bank policy rate collection beyond Tier 1
recommendations.reason: central_bank_policy_rate_pct missing for all 25 Tier 2 and all 20 Tier 3 countries. Only collected for select Tier 1 (29 of 30). Agent 01 notes acknowledged this gap.
recommendations.impact: Key monetary policy indicator for emerging market investors.

recommendations.action: Investigate Agent 09 (data_integrator) performance — 168 minutes vs 5-10 minute budget
recommendations.reason: Agent 09 took 168 minutes (2.8 hours), far exceeding its 5-10 minute time budget. This is the primary driver of pipeline duration overrun. Likely caused by first-run initialization creating 75 country files from scratch.
recommendations.impact: Reducing to budget would save ~158 minutes per run.

recommendations.action: Reduce idle time between pipeline phases
recommendations.reason: Wall-clock duration is 855.0 minutes vs 521.0 total agent processing time. Gap of 334.0 minutes of idle/scheduling time between phases. Phases 2-3 gap: 15 min. Phase 3-4 gap: 0 min. Phase 4-5 gap: 27 min. Phase 5-6 gap: 192 min (Agent 10 to 11). Phase 6-7 gap: 35 min.
20 Tier 3 countries lack narratives (AGO, AZE, BGR, CIV, COD, ETH, GEO, GHA, HRV, HUN, JOR, KEN, LBY, LKA, MMR, MOZ, SEN, SRB, TZA, UZB). Agent 13 only generates narratives for Tier 1 and Tier 2.
recommendations.impact: Improves completeness; several Tier 3 countries have active alerts (ETH critical, MMR critical).

recommendations.action: Investigate Sweden central bank rate source disagreement
recommendations.reason: ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE: SWE central_bank_policy_rate_pct shows >28.6% source disagreement with Riksbank. Should verify correct rate (1.75% vs alternative).
recommendations.impact: Ensures accuracy for a Tier 1 country monetary policy indicator.

recommendations.action: Monitor Libya GDP growth flag and Hormuz traffic collapse
recommendations.reason: Two FLAG verdicts: (1) Libya 15.6% GDP growth (extreme but plausible post-conflict recovery), (2) Strait of Hormuz -70% traffic (confirmed event). Both accepted as plausible but should be tracked.
recommendations.impact: Flags are correctly identified; monitor for updated data next week.

recommendations.action: Plan endowments layer data collection (natural resources, geography)
recommendations.reason: Layer 1 (Endowments) from the factor model spec is entirely uncollected. This includes natural resources, geography, demographics beyond basic population. These are mostly static/annual factors.
recommendations.impact: Long-term: would significantly increase coverage. Not urgent as these change slowly.

summary.headline: First run complete with strong data quality but significant coverage gaps
summary.data_quality: HIGH — 99.8% acceptance rate, 0 rejections, avg confidence 0.805
summary.coverage_status: BELOW TARGET — Tier 1: 57.6% (target 90%), Tier 2: 51.3% (target 70%), Tier 3: 50.7% (target 50%). Three entire sections (political, trade, military) are empty.
summary.coverage_of_collected_sections: Macroeconomic 77.1%, Demographic 100%, Derived 100%. Strong where data was collected.
summary.pipeline_status: OVER BUDGET — 855 minutes wall-clock (budget: 180-300 min). Primary cause: Agent 09 first-run overhead (168 min) and inter-phase idle time (334 min).
critical_issues: Political/governance indices not collected (0% for all countries)
Trade data not collected (0% for all countries)
All 30 Tier 1 countries below 80% coverage threshold
positive_findings: Average confidence 0.805 — solidly in the high range
Demographic data 100% complete for all countries
Derived metrics computed for all 75 countries
57 alerts generated with correct severity classification
All 6 escalation groups properly identified and documented
next_week_priorities: 1. Add political/governance data collection (biggest coverage gap)
4. Fix exchange rate mapping in Agent 07
5. Optimize Agent 09 for incremental updates (vs first-run)
